title: Nestlé India appoints Prateek Tripathi as Head of IS/IT & Nestlé Business Excellence - Storyboard18
content:
Tripathi, 45, joined Nestlé in 2019 and currently serves as Data Governance Lead for Zone AOA (Asia, Oceania and Africa) since February 2025.
ADVERTISEMENT
Nestlé India has announced a change in its senior management, appointing Prateek Tripathi as the new Head of IS/IT & Nestlé Business Excellence, effective April 1, 2026, subject to approvals. He will succeed Krishna Guha Roy, who will move on to take up a larger leadership role within the global Nestlé group.
The company disclosed the development in a regulatory filing to the stock exchanges under Regulation 30 of SEBI’s Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements (LODR). Roy will cease to hold her current role on March 31, 2026 as she transitions to the new global assignment within the organisation.
Tripathi, 45, joined Nestlé in 2019 and currently serves as Data Governance Lead for Zone AOA (Asia, Oceania and Africa) since February 2025. Over the years, he has played a key role in strengthening the company’s analytics and data integration capabilities across the South Asia region.
UltraTech Cement elevates Jayant Dua as managing director, to take charge in 2027
Before joining Nestlé, Tripathi held leadership roles at several major organisations including GE, Citibank, Parle Agro, and Nielsen, bringing more than 21 years of experience in analytics, data governance and business transformation.
At Nestlé, he began his journey as Data Analytics Manager and has since been instrumental in driving the company’s data governance framework, ensuring compliance with internal policies while identifying opportunities for optimisation across the Zone AOA markets.
Tripathi holds a Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics, Computer Science and Statistics from Osmania University and a Postgraduate Diploma in Business Management from Goa Institute of Management.
According to the filing, his experience in analytics, governance and digital integration positions him to lead Nestlé India’s IT and business excellence function as the company continues to scale its technology and data-led initiatives.
Want to work at Meta? CTO Andrew Bosworth says be “relentless” about great work
The company said the disclosure is being made for information and record in line with SEBI regulations governing senior management changes.
